text:
In his small town, Joe had a hobby that he shared with a few friends: crafting miniature landscapes. His room was a museum to these tiny worlds. He built entire villages and forests complete with trees so precise they looked like they were carved from wood, rocks just right to form rivers, and sometimes even tiny barnyard animals. Joe loved making rainbows out of plastic bottles.

One Friday evening, after Joe had been at his craft for hours, his phone rang. It was Karen, his longstanding friend. She'd heard about his latest creation on a podcast.

"Joe!" Karen sounded genuinely excited. "That mountain lake over there? I took a picture while hiking yesterday. I think it's totally perfect. Do you think..."

He sighed, wiping his hands on his apron. "It's okay, Karen. I don't think it'll do. See this tree?"

He pointed at a lone oak.

"It looks so lonely. What's wrong with it?"

He chuckled softly. "
